steps.title: Find the key claim
steps.content: Moreno and colleagues claim that making temporal distinctions along imagined linear axes is not ubiquitous.

steps.title: Apply the claim to the choices
steps.content:
When outdoors and referring to the future, Yupno speakers facing uphill gesture in front of themselves, whereas those facing downhill gesture behind themselves. shows Yupno speakers using the uphill/downhill axis for future gestures, which demonstrates they do not rely on a left/right axis for time.

The other choices either describe gesture frequency, hand preference, or shared grammar, none of which address whether the Yupno use a different spatial axis for time.

That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
